description_long: This shrub consists of several slender, upright stems with narrow, elongated green leaves arranged alternately along the length of each stem. The stems are thin and flexible, with a sparse distribution of leaves that become smaller and more clustered toward the top. At the apex of each stem, there is a small, subtle inflorescence or bud, giving the shrub a delicate, airy appearance. The overall structure is open and vertical, with each stem spaced apart, suggesting a light and non-dense growth habit. The coloration is predominantly green with hints of brown along the stems, and the leaves have a matte, slightly rough texture. This type of shrub is commonly found in urban landscapes, parks, or roadside plantings where a low-maintenance, decorative plant is desired.
description: A slender, upright shrub with narrow green leaves and sparse, vertical stems topped with small buds. The structure is open and airy, with a delicate, matte finish.
description_view_0: Five upright stems with narrow leaves are visible, each spaced apart and showing the open, airy structure of the shrub.
description_view_1: A single stem is seen from the front, highlighting the vertical arrangement of leaves and the small bud at the top.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, five stems are visible, but the angle shows more of the side profile of the leaves and stems.
description_view_3: A single stem is shown from the back, emphasizing the verticality and sparse leaf distribution.
